metadata.title: Insurtech & Proptech Competitive Intelligence Dashboard
metadata.source: SEC EDGAR companyfacts API, latest annual 10-K facts

companies.analysis: Lemonade remains a high-growth insurtech with revenue growth of 67.4% but a deeply negative net margin of -55.1%. Its biggest strength is brand-led digital distribution, while the key risk is operating intensity, reflected in an OpEx ratio of 153.5% and continued cash-burn pressure. Relative to REITs and specialty insurers, the company is still proving whether growth can convert into durable underwriting profit.

Hippo is the weakest financial performer in the peer set, with revenue of $209.7M and a net margin of -130.2%. Its strength is exposure to the home insurance modernization theme, but the company still carries meaningful execution risk because losses remain large relative to revenue. The industry context is unforgiving: investors are rewarding insurtech scale only when it comes with a credible path to profitability.

American Homes 4 Rent is a mature property platform with revenue of $1623.6M, growth of 8.9%, and a positive net margin of 26.6%. Its biggest strength is scale and recurring rental income, while the main risk is balance-sheet sensitivity to rates and real-estate cycles. Compared with insurtech peers, the company offers slower growth but far more stable profitability.

NMI Holdings is the strongest performer in the group, pairing a 55.6% net margin with ROA of 9.6% and conservative leverage of 0.70x. Its biggest strength is profitable specialty insurance underwriting with disciplined capital use. The main risk is cyclicality in housing and mortgage credit conditions, but its profitability profile stands well above both insurtech and REIT peers.

Invitation Homes is a mature property platform with revenue of $2432.3M, growth of 8.7%, and a positive net margin of 21.4%. Its biggest strength is scale and recurring rental income, while the main risk is balance-sheet sensitivity to rates and real-estate cycles. Compared with insurtech peers, the company offers slower growth but far more stable profitability.

Essex Property Trust is a mature property platform with revenue of $1669.4M, growth of 3.9%, and a positive net margin of 24.9%. Its biggest strength is scale and recurring rental income, while the main risk is balance-sheet sensitivity to rates and real-estate cycles. Compared with insurtech peers, the company offers slower growth but far more stable profitability.
